attacking_style: Breakaway specialist who wins from large groups (29-30 riders) with strong uphill sprint finishes. Excels in final 800m-1km after surviving climbs rather than attacking on them.
team_tactics: BORA leverages team depth to place multiple riders in breaks (Kamna partnership visible). However, hesitates to lead when numerically superior, preferring reactive positioning over proactive pulling.
terrain_preference: HC and category climbs with uphill sprint finishes suit best. Survives hard climbs 'by teeth-gritted determination' then unleashes finish speed. Struggles when required to lead/control breakaways on flatter finales.
vulnerability: Tactical indecision when holding numerical advantage—hesitates to commit when leadership required. Peloton marks him too dangerous for certain breaks, limiting opportunities when identified as GC-adjacent threat.
key_patterns: Wins from large breakaways (29-30 riders) with uphill finishes, not solo efforts (3 instances)
Survives climbs marginally then wins sprint, nearly dropped before winning twice at Giro
Peloton threat assessment limits break inclusion despite teammate allowed forward (Vuelta stage 15)
Hesitates leading when teammates present, prefers reactive tactics over controlling (Vuelta finale)
Breakthrough form windows: exceptional clusters like 2 wins in 3 days Giro 2023
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Giro peak (2 wins, age 29 breakthrough) followed by Vuelta tactical hesitation suggests form declining or confidence issues post-success. No 2024 data limits assessment.
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 15
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 21
